name: Integration Enhancement Research Docs
description: docs/research/integration-enhancement/ - 89 AWS services audit, OSS framework analysis (OpenSandbox, Strands, Cedar), MCP ecosystem deep dive (200+ servers), cost analysis, competitive positioning, integration roadmap.

name: skill-format-compatibility
description: Comprehensive skill/tool format survey across 5 agent platforms (OpenClaw, Claude Code, MCP, Strands, AgentCore): docs/research/skills/01-Platform-Skill-Formats.md. Covers format specifications, execution models, compatibility layer design, marketplace architecture. Key recommendation: SKILL.md v2 native format + MCP-first protocol + multi-platform adapters.

content: MCP + AgentCore Gateway infrastructure: Use MCP protocol (JSON-RPC 2.0) for accessing 10k+ community tools; AgentCore Gateway (GA Aug 2025) provides managed infrastructure with semantic discovery, multi-tenant isolation, IAM/OAuth auth; Hybrid architecture recommended (direct MCP for latency-sensitive tools, Gateway for shared/managed tools); Schema gap bridged via hybrid format combining JSON Schema validation with LLM-optimized triggers; Research docs: docs/research/skills/03-MCP-AgentCore-Gateway.md (1.7k lines)

name: aws-account-discovery-architecture
description: Five AWS services compose into unified account-wide discovery: (1) AWS Config for inventory/compliance/changes (5-15min lag), (2) Resource Explorer 2 for instant cross-region tag search, (3) CloudFormation for stack provenance/drift, (4) Cost Explorer for spending attribution (24h lag), (5) Tags for business context mapping. Real-time sync via EventBridge → Lambda → DynamoDB resource index enables GraphQL queries for 'living infrastructure map' with <5min update latency.

content: cost-allocation-strategies: Three models for AWS cost attribution: (1) Account-based - one account per team (zero tagging effort, account sprawl), (2) Tag-based - fine-grained resource tagging (highest accuracy, requires 100% compliance + 24h activation lag), (3) Hybrid - AWS Cost Categories + account hierarchy + tags (enterprise scale, handles edge cases). Tag-based requires proactive governance (SCPs deny untagged resource creation) vs reactive (Config rules audit compliance).

agentcore-strands-integration-wiring: Complete Chimera agent wiring sequence: BedrockAgentCoreApp + @entrypoint → extract tenant from JWT claims → load DynamoDB config → AgentCoreMemorySessionManager with namespace tenant-{id}-user-{id} → collect tools (Gateway MCPClient + Code Interpreter + Browser) → create Strands Agent(model, tools, session_manager) → stream response. Main.py pattern at docs/research/agentcore-strands/10-Chimera-Integration-Guide.md

openclaw-nemoclaw-architecture-deep-dive: OpenClaw is single-user personal assistant OS (Gateway+Pi runtime, 4 tools, Lane Queue session serialization, SKILL.md marketplace, local SQLite memory). NemoClaw is enterprise security wrapper (OpenShell sandbox, Landlock+seccomp, deny-by-default network, inference routing). Critical gap: NO multi-tenancy. Chimera differentiators: AWS-native account access (not computer), DynamoDB multi-tenant isolation, Cedar policies, 7-stage skill security, evolution safety harness.

name: dgm-paper-chimera-integration
description: Darwin Godel Machine (arxiv 2505.22954) formalizes population-based self-improvement for coding agents. Key patterns for Chimera integration: (1) agent configuration archive with lineage tracking, (2) composite fitness evaluation function, (3) diversity-aware selection avoiding local optima, (4) benchmark-based regression testing from golden datasets, (5) evolution lineage tracking. DGM uses LLM-guided mutation + empirical validation (SWE-bench 20->50%, Polyglot 14->31%). Chimera already ahead on safety (Cedar policies, rate limits, rollback). Integration opportunity: adopt DGM exploration strategy within Chimera safety envelope.
